In Lebanon, he is still celebrated as a literary hero.\", \"He is chiefly known in the English-speaking world for his 1923 book The Prophet, an early example of inspirational fiction including a series of philosophical essays written in poetic English prose. Zeb Beck is a fine author with a cracking sense of humour, and the sardonic cynicism of his anti-hero sizzles with every exchange.\\\"--Author Matt McAvoy\", \"Every weekend, Gilmore Crowell crosses the border into Tijuana\u2019s redlight district. He\u2019s not there for sex; he\u2019s there to save his failing academic career.Gil's anthropological study of Tijuana streetwalkers had his dissertation advisor cheering him on. But that was years ago, before his best sources up and vanished. Now, with no connection to the sex worker community, his research has stalled and the faculty elders are about to kick him out of the grad program. Plus, he\u2019s broke.He takes a job teaching at a juvenile detention center. The steady income gives him the means to keep making his weekly trips across the border. Now he\u2019s paying the girls to speak with him. In a sudden moment of insight, he realizes that giving them something besides cold hard cash could help him forge a new inroad to the community. But do they want what he's offering?\"]", "description": "[\"Review\", \"\u2605\u2605\u2605\u2605\u2605 \\\"The writing in The Melancholy Strumpet Master by Zeb Beck is a perfect example of how an author can balance dark humor with emotional depth. args.code:
import json, pandas as pd
perfect = pd.DataFrame(var_call_x7QTbpNzYV8WUSOumnGGDN5p)
books_path = var_call_PbwHxCqEpDIa4HiYbOr3Hp7A
import pathlib
with open(books_path, 'r') as f:
    books = json.load(f)
books_df = pd.DataFrame(books)
# filter English language from details string and Literature & Fiction in categories
mask_english = books_df['details'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False)
mask_cat = books_df['categories'].str.contains('Literature & Fiction', case=False, na=False)
lf_english = books_df[mask_english & mask_cat]
# fuzzy join purchase_id <-> book_id via simple equality on trailing numeric id
perfect['id_num'] = perfect['purchase_id'].str.extract('(\d+)$').astype(int)
lf_english['id_num'] = lf_english['book_id'].str.extract('(\d+)$').astype(int)
merged = lf_english.merge(perfect[['id_num','avg_rating']], on='id_num', how='inner')
result = merged.to_dict(orient='records')
result_json = json.dumps(result)
print("__RESULT__:")
print(result_json)

val_args.code:
import json, pandas as pd
perfect = pd.DataFrame(var_call_x7QTbpNzYV8WUSOumnGGDN5p)
books_path = var_call_PbwHxCqEpDIa4HiYbOr3Hp7A
import pathlib
with open(books_path, 'r') as f:
    books = json.load(f)
books_df = pd.DataFrame(books)
# filter English language from details string and Literature & Fiction in categories
mask_english = books_df['details'].str.contains('English', case=False, na=False)
mask_cat = books_df['categories'].str.contains('Literature & Fiction', case=False, na=False)
lf_english = books_df[mask_english & mask_cat]
# fuzzy join purchase_id <-> book_id via simple equality on trailing numeric id
perfect['id_num'] = perfect['purchase_id'].str.extract('(\d+)$').astype(int)
lf_english['id_num'] = lf_english['book_id'].str.extract('(\d+)$').astype(int)
merged = lf_english.merge(perfect[['id_num','avg_rating']], on='id_num', how='inner')
result = merged.to_dict(orient='records')
result_json = json.dumps(result)
print("__RESULT__:")
print(result_json)
